triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Boundary snapshots in evidence-dispatch.ts implement git ls-files and filesystem walk fallbacks, but untracked and untracked cached files may be missed in certain edge cases.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to capture cached, other, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore. Returns null on failure, triggering walkFiles fallback.
evidence.why: This is the primary git-backed detection mechanism. The -c flag gets cached files, -o gets other/untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks the filesystem starting from project root, excluding common directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) via excludedDir().
evidence.why: This is the non-git fallback. It walks all directories except those in the hardcoded exclusion list.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() result when available, otherwise falls back to walkFiles(). It then excludes allowedRoots (e.g., .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-*) and computes SHA-256 digests for remaining files.
evidence.why: This is the core snapshot function. Line 99 shows gitFiles || walkFiles fallback logic.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() hardcodes six directory names: .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache. Any directory matching these names is skipped during walkFiles().
evidence.why: This is the exclusion list used by the filesystem walk fallback.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) before and after agent execution, passing output_dir as the only allowed write root.
evidence.why: This shows the boundary snapshot is taken with the dispatch artifact root excluded from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' creates a git repo, has agent create untracked-mutation.txt, and verifies boundary violation is detected.
evidence.why: This test proves git ls-files with -o flag detects untracked file additions.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' runs without git, has agent modify src/existing.txt and create agent-scratch.txt in runtimeDir. Only src/existing.txt modification is detected.
evidence.why: This test proves walkFiles fallback works and correctly excludes allowed roots.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps, classifying changes as 'deleted', 'modified', or 'added' based on map entry presence and digest equality.
evidence.why: This is how mutations are classified. Modified files trigger boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Boundary result sets project_mutation to 'violated' if changes.length > 0, otherwise 'clean'. Changes array is sorted by path. Allowed roots are recorded.
evidence.why: This is how the boundary check final result is computed and surfaced.

evidence.summary: Any boundary changes are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with path, change type, and message. These blockers are added to readiness and set prd_ready to false.
evidence.why: This shows how boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agents pass.

evidence.summary: output_dir defaults to .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-{session}-{timestamp}-{random} and is passed as runtimeDir to provider prompts and allowedRoots to buildBoundarySnapshot.
evidence.why: This shows the artifact root location structure and how it's excluded from mutation detection.

assumptions: The git ls-files -co command captures all tracked, cached, and untracked files that are not in .gitignore.
The walkFiles fallback is intended for non-git projects or when git commands fail.
The excludedDir list is sufficient to exclude build/cache directories that should not be tracked.
File digest computation uses SHA-256 of full file contents; inode changes are not tracked separately.
Evidence agents run with full tools but the boundary check happens after all agents complete.
risks: Git ls-files -co may miss untracked files that are gitignored but still exist in the working tree.
Git ls-files -co may not detect files in submodules or worktrees unless explicitly included.
The walkFiles fallback excludes directories by name only, not path; a nested node_modules would be excluded but top-level node_modules/a/node_modules/b would not.
The walkFiles fallback does not honor .gitignore patterns; it would include gitignored files that exist.
The excludedDir list is hardcoded and may not cover all build/cache directories in all projects.
Symlinks are not explicitly handled; walkFiles may follow them and include or exclude based on the target path.
Empty directories are not tracked because only files are added to the snapshot map.
Concurrent modifications during agent execution (e.g., background processes) would be detected as boundary violations even if not caused by agents.
missing: Test coverage for gitignored untracked files that exist but are not captured by git ls-files -co.
Test coverage for nested excluded directories in non-git fallback (e.g., node_modules/a/node_modules/b).
Test coverage for symlink handling in walkFiles fallback.
Test coverage for submodule detection when using git ls-files.
Test coverage for worktree scenarios where git -C may behave differently.
Evidence that the walkFiles fallback correctly handles permission errors on inaccessible directories.

result.notes: Core boundary detection implementation is sound: git-backed and non-git fallbacks exist, tests pass for primary scenarios, allowed roots are correctly excluded. Missing coverage is for edge cases (gitignored files, nested exclusions, submodules, symlinks).

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is verified: boundary snapshots implement git ls-files with filesystem walk fallbacks, tests cover primary scenarios, and missing coverage is for documented edge cases.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() function runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' and returns null on failure, triggering walkFiles fallback.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer evidence line 62-69 is accurate and describes the primary git-backed detection mechanism.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks filesystem starting from project root, excluding directories matching excludedDir() names.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer evidence line 71-85 accurately describes the non-git fallback implementation.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() || walkFiles() fallback on line 99, excludes allowedRoots, computes SHA-256 digests.
evidence.why: Verifies the core snapshot function with fallback logic matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() hardcoded list: ['.git', 'node_modules', 'dist', 'coverage', '.next', '.cache'] used by walkFiles fallback.
evidence.why: Verifies the exclusion list cited in explorer evidence is accurate.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) before and after agent execution, passing output_dir as allowed write root.
evidence.why: Verifies boundary snapshot timing and allowed root exclusion matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' creates git repo, untracked-mutation.txt, verifies violation detection.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer test evidence exists and proves untracked file detection works.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' modifies src/existing.txt, creates agent-scratch.txt in runtimeDir, detects only project mutation.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer test evidence proves walkFiles fallback works and excludes allowed roots.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps, classifying changes as 'deleted', 'modified', or 'added' based on map entry presence and digest equality.
evidence.why: Verifies the mutation classification logic matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: Boundary result sets project_mutation to 'violated' if changes.length > 0, otherwise 'clean'. Changes array is sorted by path.
evidence.why: Verifies final boundary computation logic matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: Boundary changes converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with path, change type, and message. Blockers added to readiness and set prd_ready to false.
evidence.why: Verifies how boundary violations block PRD readiness matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: outputDir defaults to .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-{session}-{timestamp}-{random}, passed as runtimeDir and allowedRoots.
evidence.why: Verifies artifact root structure and exclusion matches explorer evidence.

assumptions: Explorer assumptions are reasonable given the code: git ls-files behavior, walkFiles fallback purpose, fileDigest SHA-256 computation.
risks: If the explorer claim is wrong, boundary violations could go undetected, allowing evidence agents to mutate target project files without blocking PRD readiness.

result.notes: All 11 explorer evidence records verified against actual source and test files. Line ranges are accurate. Tests exist and cover claimed scenarios. Assumptions are reasonable. Missing coverage is for legitimate edge cases. Evidence supports the claim with high confidence.
